title: D11 mock strategy is broken — `export -f has_cmd` is shadowed by install.sh's own line-21 redefinition (validated empirically on macOS bash 3.2.57)
body: Testability validated empirically: install.sh redefines has_cmd() at lines 21-25 AFTER any test export, so the local definition wins. Codex independently confirmed the shell mechanic. Whole 12-case test plan rests on this. Required swap: PATH-stub model — test prepends a dir of executable stubs (printf '#!/bin/bash\nexit 1\n' > $TMPSTUBS/brew && chmod +x). Validated against `brew bundle install`.

title: D4 owner-detect introduces semantic REGRESSION — `script_dir == git_root` would mark Justin as OWNER from anywhere
body: Current owner = PWD == REPO_DIR (where REPO_DIR is install.sh's dirname). Proposed owner = script_dir == git_root. Because Justin's install.sh lives at the git root of MonsterFlow, ANY invocation of `~/Projects/MonsterFlow/install.sh` from any cwd would resolve script_dir == git_root and mark him as OWNER — including invocations from inside an adopter project. Worse than today: today running from another cwd flips to ADOPTER mode (defensive); the new logic flips to OWNER (privacy-default-flipping). Required: combine BOTH conditions — `PWD == REPO_DIR AND script_dir == git_root` — or scope the realpath check to only resolving symlinks, not relocating. Plan calls this a 'hardening' but it's a regression for the dogfood case.

title: D8/W2-2.6 says 'wrap brew-bundle non-zero exit' but install.sh has no brew-bundle today — it's a NEW code path
body: Plan-vs-reality drift: D8 frames the catch pattern as wrapping existing behavior. install.sh actually has zero brew-bundle calls. /build must add the entire install stage from scratch (Stage 5). Implementation effort is larger than D8 implies; sub-tasks need to include: argv parse for the install confirm prompt, brew-bundle stub-friendliness, post-install detection re-run to validate the install actually fixed the missing tools, and the catch-failure messaging.

title: D6 `printf %q` is bash escaping but .zshrc is sourced by zsh — round-trip not guaranteed
body: Plan adopts `printf %q` for the .zshrc source path quoting (Security T9 mitigation). But .zshrc is sourced by zsh; bash %q escaping uses bash-specific syntax (e.g., `\$''` for control chars) that may not parse the same way under zsh. Required: use single-quote escaping (`'` and `'\\''` for embedded singles) — POSIX-portable across bash and zsh — or write a zsh-compatible literal. Otherwise a path with special chars breaks the source line silently.

title: D1 'argv parse before Linux guard' conflicts with current install.sh top-level side effects (REPO_DIR/VERSION/banner)
body: Plan says argv parsing runs before Linux guard so `--help` works on Linux. But install.sh today computes REPO_DIR (line 7), VERSION (line 9), and prints the installer banner (lines 11-15) BEFORE any flag parse could go. `install.sh --help` will still touch repo files and print the banner unless the top is restructured more aggressively than the plan admits. Required: move banner + computed-vars below the flag-parse + Linux guard block, OR have --help short-circuit before any I/O.

title: MONSTERFLOW_INSTALL_TEST recursion guard named in D3/R2/D11 but the W2 task list doesn't specify the two install.sh sites that need it
body: Plugin install (lines 305-316) and `bash tests/run-tests.sh` invocation (lines 318-326) both need short-circuits. Without the second one, tests/test-install.sh forks bash tests/run-tests.sh which forks tests/test-install.sh which forks... = fork-bomb. Required: add explicit W2 task 2.9b with 'Wrap line 305-316 plugin-install AND line 318-326 test-suite-validate behind MONSTERFLOW_INSTALL_TEST=1 short-circuit (in addition to MONSTERFLOW_NON_INTERACTIVE prompt-suppression).'

title: Brew-stub strategy not uniform across cases 1, 3a, 4 — stub state lifecycle never specified
body: Case 1 needs persistent stub state across two install.sh runs (idempotency test). Case 3a needs argv-recording stub (assert brew bundle invoked). Case 4 needs state mutation mid-test (jq missing → installed). Plan never specifies: where stub lives, how state resets between cases, how state persists within a case. Required: W4 task 4.1 ship criterion expanded to 'Brew stub binary in $BATS_TMPDIR/stubs/brew with state file at $STUB_STATE; reset between cases via setup_test().'

title: Case 7a uses `script -q /dev/null` for TTY simulation — wrong on macOS BSD `script`
body: BSD `script` (Darwin) takes `script [-q] <file> bash -c '...'` with positional file argument. There is no `-c` flag; `/dev/null` cannot be the typescript target on Darwin. The plan's case 7a TTY-presence test will silently fail or behave oddly. Required: use `expect` (`expect -c 'spawn ...'`) for TTY simulation, OR drop case 7a from automation and document it as manual-only.

title: W2 ship criterion calls for autorun-shell-reviewer subagent gate but pass threshold undefined and callable mechanism unspecified
body: Risk MF1: 'subagent gate lacks pass threshold — define High = block, Medium = document, Low = ignore; if 3+ Highs, split the wave.' Codex: 'process theater unless the build system actually supports that reviewer invocation.' Required: pin (a) threshold (recommend Risk MF1's wording), (b) WHO invokes the subagent (Justin manually before merge, or /build agent automatically? Per CLAUDE.md, autorun-shell-reviewer is on-demand only — Justin invokes it).

title: Security T1 supply-chain has no R-row in plan's risk register — config/* curl|wget|eval grep gate not wired
body: Risk MF2: T1 supply chain (compromised MonsterFlow repo) was Security's first threat but plan.md's R1-R10 doesn't include it. Required: add R11 'config/* supply chain — adversary commits malicious cmux.json/tmux.conf/zsh-prompt-colors.zsh' with mitigation 'tests/test-config-content.sh greps for curl|wget|nc|bash <(|eval against config/* and fails CI on any match.' Wave 4 task 4.6 (or new 4.7).

title: Risk MF3: brew transitive trust — D8 catches failures but doesn't preview the resolved install set before user confirms
body: Adopter sees 'About to install via Homebrew: gh, shellcheck, jq, cmux' but brew bundle pulls transitive dependencies (gh might pull go runtime). Required: parse `brew deps --include-build --formula <each-formula>` and display total resolved set + transitive-deps-summary before the [Y/n] confirm. Bounded effort (~15 line addition to install stage).

title: W5 5.3 CHANGELOG diff source-of-truth direction is inverted — install.sh treats CHANGELOG as derived, conventionally CHANGELOG is canonical
body: Plan: 'CHANGELOG.md migration bullets diff cleanly against install.sh's print_upgrade_message source.' This makes install.sh the source-of-truth. Conventionally CHANGELOG is curated. Required: pick — either flip the dependency (install.sh sources CHANGELOG.md at startup, parses out the v0.5.0 section) OR document explicitly that install.sh is canonical and CHANGELOG.md is auto-generated.

title: W2 commit count is 10 not 9 — Convergence Notes / Risk R1 / synthesis say 9, task table has 10 (2.1-2.10)
body: Sequencing flagged off-by-one. Completeness corroborated. Plan-internal inconsistency. Required: pick (a) merge two W2 commits (sequencing recommends 2.1+2.4 or 2.9+2.10) OR (b) update Convergence Notes + Risk R1 to say 10. Either is fine; just pick one.

title: Bash 3.2 (macOS default) vs 5.x export -f inheritance — pin /bin/bash invocation in test harness
body: Convergence with the mock-strategy issue: even with PATH-stub fix, the test harness must explicitly invoke /bin/bash (not /opt/homebrew/bin/bash) so all cases test against the bash version adopters actually have. Required: tests/test-install.sh uses `#!/bin/bash` shebang AND explicitly passes through `BASH=/bin/bash` to install.sh subshells.

title: Cmux requires macOS >=14 — adopter on macOS 13 hits cryptic cask install failure; plan doesn't pin macOS minimum
body: Risk SF4: Confirmed via earlier `brew info cmux` output ('Required: macOS >= 14'). Required: add pre-flight `sw_vers -productVersion` check next to Linux guard; if <14, downgrade cmux from RECOMMENDED to OPTIONAL for that adopter (or fail-fast with clear message).

title: `--non-interactive` auto-detect via `[ -t 0 ]` collides with tests piping answers via stdin
body: Risk SF5 + Codex: when tests pipe `Y\n` to install.sh's stdin, `[ -t 0 ]` returns false and install.sh enters non-interactive mode — defeating the purpose of piping answers. Required: add MONSTERFLOW_FORCE_INTERACTIVE=1 env var that overrides the [ -t 0 ] auto-detect; tests set this when they want to test prompt-driven paths.

title: link_file() existing backup pattern overwrites prior .bak — re-running theme install with existing real .tmux.conf clobbers the prior backup
body: Existing link_file() (lines 91-100): `mv $dst → ${dst}.bak`. If adopter has ~/.tmux.conf.bak from a previous backup, it gets overwritten silently. Plan relies on link_file as-is for theme safety. Required: bump backup naming to `${dst}.bak.YYYYMMDDHHMMSS` to preserve history, or refuse to overwrite an existing .bak with a clear message.

title: W1 task 1.6 'After SIGINT trap, before migration detect' is misleading — SIGINT trap is W2 (lands later)
body: Sequencing MF1: harmless in practice (sourcing python_pip writes no files) but the placement comment is wrong by wave order. Pick: move 1.6 to W2 between 2.2 and 2.3, or remove the misleading comment.

title: Wave 4 ship criterion misses `shellcheck tests/test-install.sh` — spec mandates it but plan only gates install.sh + onboard.sh
body: Spec acceptance non-test bar: 'shellcheck install.sh scripts/onboard.sh tests/test-install.sh returns 0.' Plan's W2 ship criterion runs shellcheck on install.sh; W3 on onboard.sh; W4 ship criterion missed test-install.sh. Required: add `shellcheck tests/test-install.sh` to W4 ship criterion.

title: Scope-cut C1: MONSTERFLOW_INSTALL_TEST may be redundant with MONSTERFLOW_NON_INTERACTIVE once W2 task 2.9 wraps existing prompts
body: Conflicts with check finding ch-recursionguardundefined06 (which says 2.9 doesn't cover the recursion sites). Resolution: keep MONSTERFLOW_INSTALL_TEST as a separate guard for the test-suite-validate site (line 318-326) only. NON_INTERACTIVE handles the prompt-suppression; INSTALL_TEST handles the recursion-prevention. Two distinct concerns, two distinct env vars. Reject the C1 cut.
